notes: Opening turn notes: inspected current record via get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence/read_evidence_range. Only visible evidence is case-packet market-page.txt, evidence_id ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17. It proves the proposition/rule/market metadata/source classes, not the actual closure event. Rule elements: Iran initiated; by May 24 2026 11:59 PM ET; non-weather; major closure (broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing from Iranian airspace or major Iranian airspace region, or complete suspension affecting at least two listed airports); official Iranian aviation info and consensus credible reporting are primary sources. Investigation before filing: web searches for Iran closure May 24 2026, official CAA/NOTAM, OIIX A1010/26. Leads found: Indian Express May 23 report says Iran CAA NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 closed western Tehran FIR May 22-May 25, suspended civil permits in western sector, only limited airports/daylight/fresh approval; Notamify lead for OIIX A1015/26 NOTAMR A1010/26 raw text says all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except listed, previous permissions suspended, new Iran CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights, May 23 18:21 to May 25 08:30 UTC; SafeAirspace June summary notes later partial reopening/western part remained closed and risk context; later June reports from Turkiye Today/Anadolu are not dispositive for May 24 but show source classes. Adverse/gap: one social lead alleges no official accepted A1010/26 in Iranian official data; need verify against official NOTAM archive/CAA source. Opening will not cite unadmitted web material as proof; it will frame the dispute and identify the proof needed in later evidence phases. Cross-turn journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant opening work notes. Opportunity openings:defendant; final action record_opening_statement only; evidence submission not allowed. Inspected record with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence/read_evidence_range. Only visible evidence is case packet ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17, text/plain 4971 bytes. Plaintiff opening concedes present record proves rule/evidentiary yardstick, not event occurrence.

Case-packet rule: proposition tracks rule, not platform resolution. Operative elements: Iran initiated closure; non-weather; by May 24 2026 11:59 PM ET; major closure. Major closure means broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region, generally applicable; limited delays/partial closures do not qualify; limited exceptions do not defeat a broad closure. Alternate qualifying path is non-weather complete suspension of commercial arrivals and departures affecting at least two listed airports: IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN. Foreign/airline warnings or restrictions do not suffice. Primary sources: official Iranian aviation authority info and consensus of credible reporting.

Native investigation before opening: web queries for Iran airspace closure May 24 2026 NOTAM, site:notams.aim.faa.gov Iran NOTAM May 24 2026, Reuters Iran closes airspace May 24. Leads found: SafeAirspace Iran page and OPSGROUP current operational picture describe broader 2026 restrictions, phased reopening, western Iran restrictions, and continued avoidance; search snippets/social leads suggest possible CAA.IRI NOTAM around May 22-25 concerning western airspace/prior permission. These are not admitted evidence and not relied on as record proof; they inform source targets and adverse checks.

Source map for later phases: primary target raw CAA.IRI/Iran Airports NOTAM or official notice with effective time, geography, flight classes, exceptions, and affected airports. Corroboration from reliable NOTAM repository, airport/air-navigation statements, Reuters/AP/credible reporting, flight tracking, airline cancellation data. Adverse checks: foreign advisories only, airline risk decisions, weather, VFR-only/local/partial restrictions, continued commercial operations at listed airports, or notice outside deadline. Later evidence plan: obtain raw May 22-25 NOTAM/notice; test whether it was broad enough or only western/partial; verify whether at least two listed airports had complete arrival/departure suspensions; check timing by May 24 23:59 ET and whether alleged closure was new or continuation/modification of earlier restrictions.

Opening theory: defendant does not need to prove normal operations now; plaintiff must prove every rule element. Current record establishes only the rule. Defendant should tell council that if later raw official proof establishes a broad Iranian non-weather commercial closure by the deadline, it may carry weight, but snippets, maps, foreign restrictions, and avoidance alone cannot satisfy the market rule. File opening under 5000 chars, target under 3750.

Arguments phase notes: Re-scanned get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence. Defendant opening focuses on burden, issuer, scope, timing, and partial restriction concerns. Submitted three evidence items before relying on them: (1) ev_263eb57c30ed_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-263eb57c30ed: Notamify raw NOTAM capture for OIIX A1015/26 NOTAMR A1010/26, in force 2026-05-23 18:21 UTC to 2026-05-25 08:30 UTC; all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except listed airports; listed exceptions operational sunrise-sunset; all previous permissions suspended; new Iran CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights. (2) ev_4a64799d1143_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-4a64799d1143: Abdi Media report on A1010/26, page date 2026-05-23, says western Tehran FIR closure/restrictions extended until May 25, prior permissions canceled, new Civil Aviation Organization authorization required, security/military control context. (3) ev_05dfc9131a91_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-05dfc9131a91: Siasat report on OIIX A1010/26, page date 2026-05-23, says closure of airports across western section of Tehran FIR, daylight limited operations, fresh Iran CAA approval for passenger flights, military/diplomatic context. Analysis: Timing covers market deadline because May 24 23:59 ET = May 25 03:59 UTC, inside A1015 validity. Iran initiation shown by Iran CAA permission requirement and CAA/CAO reporting. Scope major because west Tehran FIR is a major region; exceptions do not defeat closure under rule and are limited/permission-based. Commercial effect shown by civil IFR passenger flights. Non-weather shown by security/military context and absence of weather reason. Provenance caveat: Notamify is repository not official CAA; two secondary reports corroborate; official archive not captured. Adverse social lead alleging no official accepted A1010/26 remains unresolved and should be acknowledged only as source-chain limitation if needed. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant merits work notes. Opportunity arguments:defendant; final action submit_argument; evidence submission permitted. Record inspected with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. New plaintiff evidence read exactly: ev_263eb57c30ed_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-263eb57c30ed (Notamify A1015/26 replacing A1010/26), ev_4a64799d1143_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-4a64799d1143 (Abdi Media A1010/26 report), ev_05dfc9131a91_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-05dfc9131a91 (Siasat A1010/26 report). Plaintiff also offered market page as PX-1 and technical report on timing/source chain.

Evidence analysis: PX-2 is strong but not perfect: Notamify repository capture, not official Iran CAA archive. Raw text: A1015/26 NOTAMR A1010/26, OIIX/QFALC, B 2605231821 C 2605250830EST, ref A0796/26, all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except listed airports, HJ operations, all previous permissions suspended, new permission for civil IFR passenger flights from Iran CAA. PX-3/PX-4 corroborate but are secondary and likely derivative of same NOTAM/source chain. They do not prove actual flight operations/cancellations or provide official archive custody.

Native/adverse search performed: queries: "A1015/26" "A1010/26" OIIX; "ALL AIRPORTS IN WEST PART OF TEHRAN FIR"; "A0796/26" "TEHRAN FIR". Results found Notamify A1015/26 same as PX-2; Notamify A0796/26 saying Tehran FIR resumed normal operation while west part remains closed for overflights and east overflight operation subject to conditions, valid 2026-04-21 to 2026-05-25; Notamify A0947/26 saying east-part airports HJ except OIMM/OIMS/OIZH H24 and permissions from Iran CAA; later A1066/A1285 similar west-part airport closures. No official CAA.IRI archive found in quick search. A0796 is adverse to defense in part because it supports west-region overflight closure, but it also frames this as partial/resumed normal operations with east open and a continuing recovery regime. I am not submitting A0796/A0947 as defense evidence because they likely strengthen plaintiff's major-region argument more than defeat it.

Defense argument plan: concede record shows Iran CAA-linked restriction/permission regime in western Tehran FIR during deadline and non-weather context likely. Argue plaintiff still overreads the rule: PX-2 is AD airport operations and says all airports in west part closed except many listed airports; it does not itself say all commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from a major region were suspended. The alternate airport path fails because listed exceptions include OISS (Shiraz) and OIFM (Isfahan), two named airports, operating HJ, and civil IFR passenger flights may operate with new permission. Limited exception clause helps plaintiff, but council should not erase the word major or the complete-suspension airport standard. Emphasize provenance/custody and derivative corroboration. Include technical report/source ledger as attorney analysis. Offer visible evidence used for argument: market page, PX-2, PX-3, PX-4 under defense labels.

Rebuttal phase notes: Re-scanned get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence. Defendant's strongest point: PX-2/A1015 is an airport/permission regime and allegedly does not prove transit/overflight closure; alternate two-airport complete-suspension route allegedly defeated by exceptions. Targeted search for A0796/26 found Notamify page https://notamify.com/notams/OIIX/4ec67845-38c8-470a-b7cb-e32ce5f266c4. Submitted as ev_682e9dcc5947_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-682e9dcc5947. It is category ASP Airspace Restrictions, effective 2026-04-21 09:49 UTC to 2026-05-25 08:30 UTC, and raw text states: TEHRAN FIR RESUMED NORMAL OPERATION, WHILE WEST PART REMAINS CLOSED FOR OVERFLIGHTS, with eastern overflight conditions. This directly answers the defense 'transiting' gap and is cross-referenced by A1015/26, which says REF NOTAM A0796/26 before closing all airports in the west part of Tehran FIR except limited HJ operations and requiring Iran CAA permission for civil IFR passenger flights. Rebuttal plan: do not lean on alternate airport complete-suspension route; argue major-region route is satisfied by A0796 overflight closure plus A1015 airport/passenger closure/permission regime. Exceptions are limited and match the market rule's explicit carveout for pre-approved flights by Iran CAA. Preserve provenance caveat: Notamify repository, not direct CAA archive; but preponderance supported by raw cross-referenced NOTAMs and corroborating reports. Journal updated.

Defendant surrebuttal work notes. Opportunity surrebuttals:defendant. Inspected get_case/list_evidence/stat and read new plaintiff rebuttal evidence ev_682e9dcc5947_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-682e9dcc5947, Notamify A0796/26, 1964 bytes, sha256 682e9dcc59479189306395e48f8103206308ca5d42b8c28cc591f3f69252d966. A0796 raw text states: Tehran FIR resumed normal operation while west part remains closed for overflights; east part overflight operations subject to surveillance/radar, communication, ACAS, separation, SSR conditions; effective 2026-04-21 09:49 to 2026-05-25 08:30 EST. This directly answers defense merits point that plaintiff lacked transit/overflight evidence. It is damaging to defense and must be acknowledged. Remaining defense points: evidence remains repository capture rather than official CAA archive; A0796 text itself says Tehran FIR resumed normal operation except west part, so the closure is partial/geographic; A1015 airport notice still has many listed airports operating HJ and civil IFR passenger flights possible with new Iran CAA permission; alternate complete-suspension-at-two-listed-airports route still fails because OISS and OIFM appear in exception list. Plaintiff’s best argument is major-region route plus limited-exception clause. Surrebuttal should not repeat old argument or deny A0796; should preserve narrow rule-fit and weight/custody points. No new defense evidence submitted because no targeted source found that would materially defeat A0796 before deadline; further search likely cumulative.

Closing phase notes: final scan performed with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence for PX-R1. No new plaintiff evidence available in closing; no new defendant evidence beyond surrebuttal. Defendant surrebuttal concedes PX-R1 answers transit/overflight weakness and acknowledges Iran initiation/non-weather are strong. Remaining defense points: west-only partial geography, east-side operations/conditions, daylight airport exceptions, permission-based civil IFR flights, repository rather than official CAA custody. Closing plan: do not argue whole-country closure or alternate complete-suspension-at-two-airports route. Argue major Iranian airspace region route. Rule expressly includes major Iranian airspace region and limited exceptions including Iran CAA pre-approval. Evidence chain: PX-R1/A0796 says west Tehran FIR closed for overflights through deadline; PX-2/A1015 referencing A0796 says all airports in west part closed except daylight list, prior permissions suspended, new Iran CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights; PX-3/PX-4 corroborate A1010 timing, security/military context, and CAA/CAO approval requirement. Provenance limitation remains repository + secondary reporting, but preponderance is met by cross-referenced raw NOTAM captures and corroboration. Journal updated.

Defendant closing work notes. Opportunity closings:defendant; final action deliver_closing_statement only. Final record scan completed with list_evidence plus stat of key Notamify A0796 and A1015 evidence. Evidence list contains market packet, three plaintiff argument evidence captures (A1015 Notamify, Abdi, Siasat), and one plaintiff rebuttal capture (A0796 Notamify). No new evidence after surrebuttal; plaintiff closing filed. Closing phase allows no evidence submission or technical reports.

Assessment for closing: Defense position is uphill after PX-R1/A0796. Strongest plaintiff path: market allows major region; A0796 proves west Tehran FIR closed to overflights through deadline; A1015 proves west Tehran FIR airport closures except daylight listed airports with CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights; secondary reports corroborate non-weather/security context and CAA/CAO involvement. Remaining defense points: rule excludes partial closures; A0796 says Tehran FIR resumed normal operation except west, with east open subject to conditions; A1015 lists many exceptions including named airports and allows civil IFR passenger flights with new permission; no official CAA archive, flight-tracking data, airline cancellation data, or operational proof beyond repository + secondary reports. Closing should not overclaim. It should ask council to apply a strict reading of major closure and complete-suspension requirements, while acknowledging that plaintiff wins if the council treats west Tehran FIR as a major region and the exceptions as limited exceptions to a broad regional closure.
